title: Module 6.4: Securing Outdoor Laser Shows - Assessment
description: Assessment of knowledge for outdoor laser show security procedures

questions.text: How many days before an outdoor laser show should FAA Form 7140-1 be submitted to the appropriate Service Center?
questions.explanation: FAA AC 70-1B recommends submitting Form 7140-1 at least 30 days before the planned operation to allow adequate time for review.

questions.text: At what sustained wind speed should you consider delaying or shutting down an outdoor laser show?
questions.explanation: Sustained winds above 25 mph are approaching the threshold where equipment stability and beam control become safety concerns.

questions.text: What is the minimum recommended overlap between adjacent observer coverage zones?
questions.explanation: A 30° overlap between adjacent observer coverage zones ensures no gaps in airspace monitoring.

questions.text: During a show, an observer reports an aircraft 2 miles out. What is the FIRST action the laser operator must take?

options.text: Ask for more details about the aircraft
Redirect the beams to avoid the aircraft

questions.explanation: The emergency stop must be activated IMMEDIATELY upon any aircraft report. Details and confirmation come after shutdown.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT part of the T-1 hour final check?
questions.explanation: Equipment inventory should be completed during setup (T-4 hours), not during final checks. The T-1 hour check focuses on operational readiness.

questions.text: Lightning is detected 8 miles from the venue and approaching. What is the correct decision?

options.text: Continue monitoring - lightning is still distant

questions.explanation: Lightning within 10 miles requires immediate shutdown and evacuation of outdoor areas. The 30-minute rule applies after the last thunder.

questions.text: How many safety observers are required minimum for a single laser projection site with 360° airspace exposure?
questions.explanation: A minimum of 2 observers are required for 360° coverage, with each observer responsible for approximately 180° of coverage with overlap.

questions.text: It's 30 minutes before showtime. Wind is 22 mph with gusts to 32 mph, forecast to worsen. The promoter is pressuring you to start on time. What should you do?

options.text: Start the show as scheduled - the promoter is paying
Delay and reassess after checking updated weather
Reduce beam power and start on time

questions.explanation: The prudent decision is to delay and gather more information before making a final decision. Safety always takes priority over schedule.

questions.text: What is the minimum visibility required for safe outdoor laser operations?
questions.explanation: Visibility below 3 miles compromises the ability of observers to detect aircraft and requires immediate shutdown.

questions.text: An observer calls in sick 1 hour before showtime. You have 3 observers for a site that requires 360° coverage. What is the best course of action?

options.text: Proceed with 3 observers covering larger sectors
Redeploy to optimize coverage and increase vigilance
Have a non-trained crew member fill in

questions.explanation: With one observer down, redeploy the remaining 3 to optimize coverage (120° each with overlap) and increase vigilance. This maintains safety while allowing the show to proceed.

questions.text: Which of the following items are considered CRITICAL on the pre-show checklist? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Critical items include FAA approval, emergency systems testing, sky scans, and weather assessment. Spare cables and catering are important but not safety-critical.

questions.text: Safety observers should look directly at the laser beams to verify they are pointing correctly.
questions.explanation: Observers must NEVER look directly at laser beams. They scan the airspace for aircraft, not at the beams themselves.

questions.text: When lightning is detected within ______ miles of the venue, immediate shutdown and evacuation of outdoor areas is required.
questions.explanation: The 10-mile lightning safety rule requires immediate shutdown when lightning is within 10 miles of the operation.

questions.text: After an emergency shutdown due to aircraft, when can the show resume?
questions.explanation: Restart requires confirmation from ALL observers that airspace is clear. ATC notification is good practice but not required for restart.

questions.text: Match the weather condition with the appropriate action:
questions.explanation: Understanding the thresholds for different weather actions is critical for safe operations.
